claim: IndyDevDan ships hands-on Claude Code agentic engineering content (sub-agents, hooks, multi-agent observability, Task System) framed as anti-hype tactical engineering rather than news commentary.
quote: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced) ... One Agent Is NOT ENOUGH: Agentic Coding BEYOND Claude Code ... Agentic Coding ENDGAME: Build your Claude Code SDK Custom Agents

claim: IndyDevDan explicitly positions his work around 'signal of value creation' and recommends building custom agents as the highest-ROI bet — and his Tactical Agentic Coding course generalises beyond Claude Code to Cursor, Windsurf, Cline.
quote: cancel out the noise and focus on the signal of value creation ... If you had to pick one bet, I'd recommend this: Custom Agents Above All. When it comes to signal, when it comes to value, this is the highest return on investment.

claim: IndyDevDan (disler) publishes companion open-source repos like claude-code-hooks-multi-agent-observability, evidence the on-camera builds are real, runnable artefacts rather than slideware.
quote: Real-time monitoring for Claude Code agents through simple hook event tracking.

claim: Cole Medin is a generative-AI specialist whose channel covers AI agents, RAG, local-LLM deployments, and an explicitly mixed code / no-code stack (LangChain, LangGraph, n8n, Ollama, Supabase, Open WebUI).
quote: Generative AI specialist with a wide range of experience developing AI Agents, RAG solutions, local AI deployments ... I focus on both code and no code solutions because I am a firm believer that there is a time and place for both!

claim: Cole Medin's recent series moves past surface 'vibe coding' into context engineering and RAG patterns for production agents, the kind of follow-along build content that ships working pipelines.
quote: Stop Vibe Coding: Context Engineering & RAG for AI Agents [# 12 Cole Medin]

claim: Sam Witteveen runs a long-running deep-learning channel that has become the go-to walkthrough source for agent frameworks — CrewAI, SmolAgents, Pydantic AI, LangGraph, Google's A2A protocol, Microsoft's Magentic-One.
quote: Master CrewAI - Ultimate Beginner's Guide ... SmolaAgents - Introduction to HuggingFace's Agent Framework ... Pydantic AI - Building LLM Apps and AI Agents ... Google's NEW Agent2Agent Protocol

claim: Sam Witteveen has 11 years in deep learning, 5+ years on transformers/LLMs, and has been working on autonomous agents since early 2023 — credibility that shows up as framework-by-framework hands-on tutorials rather than reaction videos.
quote: worked with Deep Learning for 11 years and with Transformers and LLMs for 5+ years, and has been working on Autonomous Agents since early 2023

claim: Mervin Praison ships an actual open-source agent framework (PraisonAI) and his channel doubles as the docs/tutorial layer for it — high signal for that specific stack but somewhat self-promotional.
quote: PraisonAI — Hire a 24/7 AI Workforce. Stop writing boilerplate and start shipping autonomous agents that research, plan, code, and execute tasks. Deployed in 5 lines of code with built-in memory, RAG, and support for 100+ LLMs.

claim: Riley Brown is the de-facto face of 'vibe coding' on YouTube — voice-driven Cursor Composer builds aimed at non-coders, useful as a barometer of what designers/founders can realistically ship without typing code.
quote: Vibe Coding: The act of using only your voice in Cursor Composer to build apps, forgetting the code even exists

claim: David Ondrej (~321k subs, ~40-min videos twice a week) leans hard into 'make money with AI' framing — practical agent/app demos but optimised more for monetisation pitches than engineering depth.
quote: videos focus on how to make money using AI tools, covering everything from building apps to using AI agents and how to monetize them ... averaging around 40 minutes, and the channel uploads about twice a week

claim: Matthew Berman (480k+ subs, ~4 vids/wk, 57M views) is broad AI-news + model-comparison territory — useful for tracking releases but lighter on actually-built agents than the IndyDevDan / Cole Medin tier.
quote: Artificial Intelligence, Open Source technology, Generative Art, and Machine Learning ... over 480,000 subscribers and more than 57 million total views ... averaging four videos per week

claim: A 2026 round-up of 'AI YouTubers we're actually watching' clusters the field: Berman / Wolfe / Wes Roth as news, Nate Herk and Nick Saraev as the n8n/Make automation builders, Greg Isenberg as AI-startup-ideas, AI Explained for research depth.
quote: Matthew Berman ... covers open-source models, LLMs, coding tutorials, and model comparisons ... Nick Saraev ... builds complex automation systems ... Make.com and n8n workflows, AI agents ... Greg Isenberg ... talks about how to build businesses with it

claim: Other build-with-AI channels worth knowing in this wave include Riley Brown (real apps with AI tools), VoloBuilds (developer-focused modern AI patterns), AI Code King (daily new dev tools), and AI Foundations (no-code AI tools through native UIs).
quote: Riley Brown ... building real apps with AI tools. The videos range from 8-minute quick builds to hour-long deep dives ... VoloBuilds ... delivers deep developer-focused content focused on modern AI development patterns ... AI Foundations ... shows how to master AI tools without coding

claim: Matt Wolfe (925k+ subs, FutureTools.io, ~250k newsletter subs) is the canonical weekly AI-news aggregator — high recall on releases, but explicitly news/curation rather than on-camera agent building.
quote: His channel focuses on breaking down the latest AI news ... his goal is to be the one place you can go once a week to stay on top of the most important AI releases and updates, without the overwhelm ... over 925,000 subscribers
